slides.title: Melatonin is a *timing signal*, not a sleeping pill.
slides.sub: A small dose, taken early, can gently nudge your body clock. The trick is when and how much.

slides.title: A clock cue, not a sedative
slides.body: Melatonin tells your body that night is coming. It *shifts your rhythm* rather than knocking you out like a sleeping pill.

slides.title: Timing does the work
slides.body: Taken a few hours before bed, a low dose *can advance your clock* so sleepiness arrives at the hour you actually want.

slides.title: More is not better
slides.body: Mega-doses can overshoot and *blunt the signal*. Studies suggest small amounts, often well under 1mg, may do the job.

slides.title: It guides, it doesn't force
slides.body: Light, late screens and caffeine can drown out the cue. Melatonin works best *alongside steady habits*, not in place of them.

slides.title: A clock you can trust
slides.body: Get the timing right and your evenings settle. The same nudge *is linked to* falling asleep sooner and waking more rested.

slides.title: See what *actually* moves your sleep.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep, food, movement and steps, then turns the patterns into one daily Health Score so you can see what timing changes really help.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Melatonin is not a sleeping pill. It is a timing signal that tells your body the night has started. 🌙😴

A small dose taken a few hours before bed can gently shift your clock, while mega-doses tend to blunt the effect. Save this for your next pharmacy run. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your sleep, food and movement into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Melatonin Timing and Dose: The Sleep Mistake Most People Make
